name: Reconstruct occluded area using context matching
Fix repeating patterns in bordered panels
Billiard Ball Diagonal Rays
rule_summary: Diagonal rays from L-shapes bounce off lines/pixels like billiard balls.

name: Repeating pattern extension
rule_summary: Extend a repeating tile pattern to fill the full output grid dimensions.

name: Plus Pattern Detection
rule_summary: Find cells where center + all 4 orthogonal neighbors are yellow (4). Replace entire plus shape with azure (8).
key_insight: A 'plus' pattern = 5 yellow cells in cross shape. All such patterns get recolored to azure.

name: Shape portal stitching
rule_summary: Two yellow shapes separated by blue gap with orange port markers. Translate mobile shape so port-face aligns with base orange. Crop to bounding box.

name: Fixed 20x20 pattern with rotation and color remapping
rule_summary: Apply one of 8 orientations (rotation × flip) and remap colors to match a fixed 20×20 reference pattern.

name: Box Slide & Diagonal Fill
rule_summary: Black-bordered boxes slide toward paired maroon rail lines. Each step of movement adds 1 orange cell to the interior, filling perpendicular to the movement direction. Fully-orange boxes don't move.
key_insight: Movement = min(distance_between_rails, grey_cells_remaining). Fill sweeps perpendicular to movement (row-by-row for vertical, column-by-column for horizontal) with counter-clockwise rotation for within-slice ordering.
colors_used: Magenta (6) background, Black (0) borders, Grey (5) unfilled, Orange (7) filled, Maroon (9) rails

name: Staircase Interior Fill
rule_summary: Nested staircase shapes form boundaries. Blue (color 1) is redistributed from incorrect positions to fill the interior gaps correctly. Total blue count is conserved.
key_insight: Two types of fill: TYPE A = gaps between same-color walls (always filled), TYPE B = extensions toward the open side of the staircase (filled bottom-up until blue quota met).

name: Extract & Stack Bordered Blocks
rule_summary: Extract blocks from bordered grid, rotate if vertical, stack centered.

name: Bar-row diagonal line propagation
rule_summary: Replace 7-triplets with 8-6-8 in data rows, propagate diagonal 6-lines through bar rows based on 3-markers.

name: Pair Frame & Solid Blocks
rule_summary: Pair frame blocks with solid blocks sorted by row position.

name: Expand cross arms with matching-color shapes
Staircase Extent Sorting
rule_summary: Vertical colored lines cross a horizontal purple line. Sort line extents ascending left-to-right.

name: Polygon vertex connection with companion shadow
rule_summary: Connect polygon vertices and add offset shadow/companion polygon.

name: Connect adjacent boxes based on key sequence
rule_summary: Grid has blue-bordered boxes with colored centers, plus a key row at the bottom. The key sequence defines consecutive color pairs (A,B) meaning "extend A toward B". For each pair, fill the gap between the A and B boxes with color A.

name: Extend diamond tails to grid edge
rule_summary: Rule: - Grid has 2+ diamond/cross shapes made of 1s on a background of 8s - Each diamond has a colored "tail" extending in one direction - Some tails are complete (reach the grid edge); others are short - Short tails are extended to the grid edge by repeating the pattern cyclically

name: Puzzle 5961cc34
rule_summary: Pattern: Diamond shapes connected by directional lines from a yellow/green marker. - Input has hexagonal diamond shapes (color 1/red) on background (color 8/blue) - Each diamond has green3 (color 3) arrow cells on one side indicating connection direction - A yellow (4) marker with green (2) line seg

name: Panel combination guided by dividing line
rule_summary: The 7x25 grid has 4 panels of 5x5 separated by a border color. Panel 1 defines a dividing line that splits the 5x5 area into two regions. The smaller region is filled with Panel 2's pattern, the larger with Panel 3's. On the dividing line itself, Panel 2 takes priority if non-background, else Panel

name: Straighten diagonal connections into right angles
rule_summary: Rule: - FG cells form line segments (horizontal/vertical) with some diagonal shortcuts - Diagonal cells (FG with no h/v FG neighbors, only diagonal FG neighbors) are removed - Gaps in lines that were bridged by the diagonals are filled with FG - Gap is filled only if its length <= diagonal chain len

name: Wall-Anchor Perpendicular Distance Profile Reflection
rule_summary: Three roles: WALL (straight line, static), MOVER (bent shape, repositioned), ANCHOR (static reference). The mover's distance profile from the wall is reflected through the anchor's position.
key_insight: The mover extends from a junction point on the wall. In the output, it extends in the OPPOSITE direction (toward the anchor) following a specific perpendicular distance profile: linear interpolation to the anchor, then matching anchor distance + 1, then decreasing.
grid_sizes: Variable (test cases have multiple independent triplets)

name: Template holes filled by colored blocks at 2× scale
rule_summary: The input contains: 1. A large colored rectangular template with black (0) holes inside 2. Several colored blocks (each made of 2×2 sub-blocks) on the black background 3. Scattered single-cell noise markers (ignored) The output scales the template by 2× (each cell → 2×2 block) and fills each hole re

name: Fill frame interiors connected to dot through chain network
rule_summary: Pattern: - Grid has rectangular/diamond frames made of colored cells (red, white, green) - Frame corners are bg cells or connecting-color cells (blue/green) - Chains of connecting-color cells link frames together - A uniquely-colored dot sits in the network - Rule: fill interior of frames connected

name: Extending short teeth of comb-like structures
rule_summary: Each structure has a bar (spine) with perpendicular teeth of varying lengths. Short teeth aligned with red dots/blocks get extended: - RED junction fills the "missing" tooth portion - BLACK (0) bar extends from junction to the red dot All unaligned red cells are removed (set to background).

name: Vertical Bar Selection by Marker Height
rule_summary: Vertical bars with colored endpoints and filler. A lone marker's vertical height selects which bar (from outermost) gets its filler replaced by endpoint color.
key_insight: Bars are grouped by endpoint color. Lone markers (same color, not part of bar) have a height that indexes which bar to convert, counting from the outermost.

name: Diagonal Symmetry Fill
rule_summary: Fill symmetric diagonal pattern across axis of symmetry. Each colored cell is reflected diagonally, and gaps in the reflected region are filled with the cell's color.
key_insight: The grid has a diagonal axis. Each non-background cell implies a symmetric counterpart across this axis. Missing counterparts are filled in.

name: Template Tiling
rule_summary: Input has 4 sections separated by 3 lines of a separator color: Section 0: Template pattern (bordered with bg, interior = binary pattern) Section 1: Mark cells (count / 2 = number of tiles) Section 2: Solid color for "filled" template cells Section 3: Solid color for "empty" template cells and gaps

name: 3x3 Block Pointer Chains
rule_summary: 3x3 blocks with center pointers form chains. Alternate keep/remove from start. Kept blocks update center to pointed-to block's center.

name: Decorated frame extension with center fill
rule_summary: 9×9 decorated frame with 3-cell borders: extend outward by placing 180°-rotated copies, fill center hole with inner ring pattern.

name: Blue Diagonal Purple Perpendicular
rule_summary: Blue dot pairs on 45° diagonals get connected. Purple dots on the blue path generate perpendicular diagonals to grid boundaries.

name: Frame Fill via Color Key
rule_summary: Color key maps frame colors to fill colors. Interior cells enclosed by frames get filled with the mapped color.

name: Maroon Dot Ring Migration
rule_summary: Maroon dots migrate into 3x3 green ring. Count reachable maroon via flood fill through non-purple walls. Fill ring cells top-outward.

name: Puzzle e3721c99
rule_summary: Pattern: Topological hole counting with template color mapping. The grid has a template region (top rows) with colored rectangles showing different numbers of interior holes (0, 1, 2, 3, ...). Below a separator line, white (color 5) shapes are scattered. Each white connected component's topological

name: 3x3 Tile Grid with Maroon Center
rule_summary: 6x6 input defines a 3x3 tile (mod 3) which tiles across 16x16 output. Center 6x6 region replaces black with maroon.
key_insight: Non-bg cells at (r%3, c%3) define a 3x3 tile. The tile offset is determined by the pattern center. Center region gets maroon highlighting.

name: Nested curve topology
rule_summary: Nested curves with color mapping based on topological nesting depth and curve orientation

name: Arrow-directed color remapping
rule_summary: T-shaped arrows around a colored grid indicate color swaps; apply swaps to recolor the grid regions
